taskDescription: Arrange the steps for examining the skull in the correct sequence, from establishing orientation to identifying deep structures.
paragraphs: Place the skull in anatomical position using the Frankfurt plane (lower orbital margin to upper border of external acoustic meatus horizontal).
Identify the four major sutures: coronal, sagittal, lambdoid, and squamous.
Examine the five normae systematically: verticalis (superior), frontalis (anterior), lateralis (lateral), occipitalis (posterior), and basalis (inferior).
Locate the pterion on the lateral view — the thinnest part of the skull where the middle meningeal artery is at risk.
Turn the skull over to examine norma basalis and identify the three cranial fossae: anterior, middle, and posterior.
Identify the major foramina and the structures passing through them: foramen magnum (brainstem), foramen ovale (CN V3), foramen spinosum (middle meningeal artery).

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
Good effort — review the areas you missed.
Excellent work! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
